steps.title: Identify the y-intercept
steps.content: The point $(0, 5)$ tells us that when $x = 0$, we have $y = 5$. This is the y-intercept: $b = 5$.

steps.title: Write the equation using slope-intercept form
steps.content:
With slope $m = \frac{1}{4}$ and y-intercept $b = 5$, substitute into the form to get:
$$f(x) = \frac{1}{4}x + 5$$
This matches choice A — A is correct.
